name: Fenugreek | ফেনুগ্রিক | Indications, Pharmacology, Dosage, Side Effects & other Generic info with Available Brand names in Bangladesh
indications: Fenugreek capsule is indicated for the treatment and prevention of- Hypoandrogenism Loss of Libido General Weakness Muscle Weakness Male Infertility
pharmacology: Fenugreek (Trigonella foenum graecum) is an annual herb native to the Mediterranean region. Fenugreek seed extract contains over 100 phytochemical constituents, including saponin glycosides (furostanol saponin and steroidal saponin). These saponin glycosides help to balance testosterone level in the body. Fenugreek also supports healthy sexual desire and helps to reduce the symptoms of andropause.
dosage_&amp;_administration: Orally 2 capsules daily with water or as advised by the physician. Duration of the treatment should be at least 3 months or as advised by the physician.
interaction: Concomitant use of Fenugreek may potentiate the effect of antidiabetic, insulin and anticoagulant drugs.
contraindications: Contraindicated in patients with known hypersensitivity to Fenugreek.
side_effects: Generally well-tolerated in the recommended dose. Occasionally may cause gastrointestinal discomfort, allergic reaction, hypoglycemia, blurred vision and nausea.
pregnancy_&amp;_lactation: Sufficient clinical data is not available for use during pregnancy and lactation.
storage_conditions: Do not store above 30 C. Protect from light moisture. Keep out of reach of children.
